samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data.</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data.</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="cnt_8">8</parameter>
        <parameter name="cnt_0">0</parameter>
        <parameter name="valid">1</parameter>
        <parameter name="invalid">0</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="cnt" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[3:0]">Counter for the number of serial inputs received.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_valid" type="sequential_logic">Valid signal for the parallel output.</reg>
        <reg id="dout_parallel" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module utilizes a 4-bit counter (cnt) to keep track of the number of serial input data received. Every eight din_serial input, dout_parallel will output. When all 8 serial data bits have been received, the valid output signal is set to 1, indicating that the parallel output data is valid. Otherwise, the valid output signal is set to 0, indicating that the parallel output data is not valid.
    </implementation>
</module>
